_comment: CI builder hardware -> lit feature table for offload-test-suite.
This is the single source of truth for the hardware-derived lit features
(AVX512, Intel-Gen-Current, AppleM<N>, ...) that monitor_failures.py uses to
evaluate XFAIL clauses when classifying XPASSes. Keep it in sync with
offload-test-suite/docs/CI.md.
TO ADD OR UPDATE A RUNNER: add/edit an entry under "runners", keyed by the
machine's `Runner name:` (as it appears in the workflow log). For each:
cpu / gpu     - human description. `cpu` also determines the host arch:
Apple* -> macOS, Snapdragon/ARM64/aarch64/Ampere -> ARM64,
otherwise x64. Override with an explicit "host" field if
the CPU string can't be classified.
cpu_features  - lit features from the CPU (e.g. AVX512). These apply to
every device tested on the machine.
gpus          - map of each device (gpu-axis) token this machine tests to
ITS OWN lit features. A machine tests its physical GPU plus
any software renderers scheduled on it (Warp / Lavapipe),
and each has distinct features: the discrete GPU may add
Intel-Gen-Current / AppleM4, while Warp / Lavapipe add none.
Token is one of AMD/NVIDIA/Intel/QC/Warp/Lavapipe/Metal.
The host arch comes from the CPU, so it isn't repeated here.
Used to resolve a workflow to its runner (and the device's
features) when the log's runner name isn't available yet.

HLSLPC-INTEL01.gpu: Intel Arc Pro B50 (Xe HPG => Intel Gen11-14/Xe)

HLSLPC-NVIDIA01.cpu: Intel Core i5-14400F (Raptor Lake refresh; consumer AVX-512 fused off)
